pattern: Reducing the two strings to the sorted positions where they differ turns the problem into pairing those positions up as cheaply as possible, where any pair can be closed at a flat price or, if the two positions are close together, at the price of the gap between them, which a small two-state scan resolves in one pass.
hints: Only the positions where the two strings disagree matter at all; if there is an odd number of them, no sequence of operations can ever make the strings equal.

Two disagreeing positions can always be resolved together for a flat price regardless of how far apart they are, or, if they happen to be close together, for a price equal to the distance between them, whichever is cheaper.

Scan the sorted disagreeing positions left to right keeping track of whether there's a single still-unpaired position left behind; at each step either park the current position to be closed later at the flat price, close a parked one against the current position, or close the current position together with the very next one directly.

edge_inputs.description: a single disagreement can never be fixed, regardless of cost
two adjacent disagreeing positions where the cheap adjacent operation is at least as good as the flat one
two adjacent disagreeing positions with a flat cost that happens to match the adjacent cost
an odd number of disagreements hidden inside a longer string, so it stays impossible however cheap the flat cost is
every position disagrees, forcing the pairing to be decided across the whole string
two adjacent disagreeing positions where the flat cost is at its maximum, so the adjacent operation is always preferred
four disagreeing positions arranged so that pairing the middle two together beats pairing every position with its immediate neighbor

stress.description: random binary strings with a random even number of disagreements and a random flat cost
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 500))
    x = rng.randint(1, 500)
    s2 = ''.join(rng.choice('01') for _ in range(n))
    s1 = list(s2)
    max_pairs = n // 2
    k = rng.randint(0, max_pairs) * 2
    positions = rng.sample(range(n), k) if k > 0 else []
    for p in positions:
        s1[p] = '1' if s1[p] == '0' else '0'
    return [''.join(s1), s2, x]

stress.description: fully disagreeing strings of maximum even length with a random flat cost
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 500))
    if n % 2 == 1:
        n -= 1
    x = rng.randint(1, 500)
    s2 = ''.join(rng.choice('01') for _ in range(n))
    s1 = ''.join('1' if c == '0' else '0' for c in s2)
    return [s1, s2, x]
